goal: Add a Replicator domain randomizer on /World/Camera's pose with uniform offset box ±(0.2, 0.2, 0.1)m around its current position for sim-to-real training data. Verify via count_prims_under_path that Replicator authored nodes.
thoughts: Replicator domain randomization runs per-sample in the render loop, unlike a one-shot random at import. add_domain_randomizer is the dedicated tool — do NOT generate raw Replicator Python ('with rep.trigger.on_frame(...)') when the tool exists. Verify that Replicator authored something under /Replicator or similar after the call.
code:
# 1. Confirm target camera present
prim_exists(prim_path="/World/Camera")

# 2. Wire the randomizer (uniform offset on pose, per-sample)
add_domain_randomizer(
    target_path="/World/Camera",
    attribute="pose",
    distribution="uniform",
    low=[-0.2, -0.2, -0.1],
    high=[0.2, 0.2, 0.1]
)

# 3. Verify the randomizer was authored
count_prims_under_path(parent_path="/Replicator", recursive=True)
failure_modes: Using Python random.uniform at import time instead of Replicator — only randomizes once, not per frame.
Specifying absolute coordinates instead of offsets — uniform([0.2, 0.2, 0.1], [0.2, 0.2, 0.1]) is a single point.
Inventing an 'inputs:randomize' attribute on the camera directly — that's not how Replicator works.
Using a range too large (±1m) that places the camera outside the valid frustum of the scene.
